name: GitHub Public Governance Example
description: Public-safe example for recording GitHub governance boundaries. This is not a connector, API client, credential store, permission grant, release tool, scan result, or automatic execution path.

access.credential_policy: Do not store GitHub tokens, passwords, API keys, cookies, private keys, session values, or secrets in this profile.

required_permissions: unknown until user approval and host Runtime evidence confirm the task scope
allowed_operations: Read public repository metadata when user-approved and current host Runtime evidence confirms access.
Read public issue or pull request metadata when user-approved and current host Runtime evidence confirms access.
Use public-safe repository facts as AI judgement input for approval and verification planning.
forbidden_operations: Do not write issues, comments, labels, branches, commits, tags, releases, secrets, settings, or permissions without explicit user approval and current host Runtime evidence.
Do not delete or modify repository data from this profile.
Do not read private repositories, private organization data, or secrets from this example.
Do not treat this profile as a GitHub connector, API client, background sync job, scanner, validator, or release automation.

external_writes.notes: A release, tag, issue edit, repository setting change, or any other external write requires explicit user approval and execution evidence outside this profile.

verified: This file is a public-safe example with placeholder repository example-org/example-repo.
not_run: No GitHub API call was made.
No connector, token, Release, tag, issue, pull request, repository setting, or secret was accessed.
missing: Real repository access, permissions, and write capability require task-specific user approval and host Runtime evidence.
